q: The study of interference and diffraction deals with geometrical optics.
Coherent sources must have the same frequency.
The secondary wavelets have the same frequency as the primary wavelets.
A diffraction grating consists of a large number of equally spaced parallel slits.
The ray approximation of geometrical optics is valid for the interference of light.
The wave model of light is necessary to explain interference and diffraction.
Constructive interference occurs when the path difference is an integer multiple of the wavelength.
If the phase difference is 0, 2π, 4π, 6π, the two positions are in phase.
The secondary wavelets spread out in all directions at the same speed as the primary wavelet.
The wavefront is always parallel to the direction of propagation.
If the path difference is λ/2, 3λ/2, 5λ/2, it is said to be completely out of phase.
Light waves are longitudinal waves.
In Young's experiment, the central fringe is always bright.
Diffraction is the bending of light around the corners of an obstacle.
The study of physical optics includes the wave characteristics of light.
Destructive interference occurs when the path difference is an odd multiple of λ/2.
The phase angle is one of the important characteristics of the periodic wave.
Newton's rings are an example of interference in thin films.
If the phase difference is π, 3π, 5π, the two positions are in phase.
The central maximum in a single-slit diffraction pattern is twice as wide as the secondary maxima.
Interference is the superposition of two or more waves.
Two light sources can be coherent if they are independent light bulbs.
For diffraction to be significant, the size of the slit must be comparable to the wavelength of light.
The width of the interference fringes increases if the distance between the slits is increased.
The fringes produced by a diffraction grating are much sharper than those produced by two slits.
A wavefront is a surface of constant phase.
Geometrical optics is also known as wave optics.
The secondary wavelets have the same speed as the primary wavelets.
The study of interference deals with wave optics.
If the path difference is 0, λ, 2λ, 3λ, it is said to be in phase.
A point source of light produces a plane wavefront.
Huygens's principle can be used to derive the law of reflection.
The ray approximation is valid when the wavelength is very large compared to the opening.
Huygens's principle can be used to derive the law of refraction.
In interference, the energy is redistributed.
The condition for constructive interference is d sin θ = mλ.
The central fringe in the interference pattern of white light is colored.
A soap bubble appears colored due to diffraction of light.
The resolving power of an optical instrument depends on diffraction.
Diffraction fringes are all of the same intensity.
Polarization is a property of longitudinal waves.
The distance between two consecutive bright fringes is called fringe width.
The fringe width is directly proportional to the wavelength of light.
Thin film interference depends on the thickness of the film.
An oil film on water shows colors due to interference.
The number of lines per unit length of a grating is the grating constant.
Light is an electromagnetic wave.
The frequency of light changes when it travels from one medium to another.
Interference occurs for both transverse and longitudinal waves.
The color of light is determined by its frequency.
